Title: Dimerization of PtrMYB074 and PtrWRKY19 mediates transcriptional activation of PtrbHLH186 for secondary xylem development in Populus trichocarpa

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: PtrMYB074 is woody dicot-specific and part of a PtrSND1-directed transcriptional regulatory network for wood formation; in this study it shares only 15 common ChIP-seq targets with PtrWRKY19, indicating highly specific combinatorial regulation when in heterodimer with PtrWRKY19.
Plant_Genes.Omics_Data: ChIP-seq of PtrMYB074-FLAG in SDX tissue (four libraries: ChIP-DNA and input DNA, two biological replicates each) mapped to P. trichocarpa genome v.3.0; data deposited in NCBI SRA under SRR15246566–SRR15246573.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts, together with PtrWRKY19, as an essential positive regulator of PtrbHLH186 expression in stem-differentiating xylem, thereby contributing to a regulatory pathway that enhances lignification, alters vessel and fibre development and confers drought-tolerance phenotypes via downstream PtrbHLH186.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: PtrMYB074 forms a heterodimer with PtrWRKY19; it does not directly bind the W-box-containing PtrbHLH186 promoter fragment in EMSA, but is recruited to the PtrbHLH186 promoter via PtrWRKY19. The PtrMYB074–PtrWRKY19 dimer is required for effective transcriptional activation of PtrbHLH186.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Component of a PtrMYB074–PtrWRKY19–PtrbHLH186 transcriptional regulatory module controlling secondary xylem development and wood formation; PtrWRKY19 binds the W-box in the PtrbHLH186 promoter and recruits PtrMYB074, and the heterodimer is required to activate PtrbHLH186 transcription.
Plant_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Regulation of lignin and secondary cell wall biosynthesis through control of downstream transcription factor PtrbHLH186 and its monolignol pathway targets.
Plant_Genes.Upstream_or_Downstream_Regulation: PtrWRKY19 --(protein–protein interaction recruits)--> PtrMYB074; PtrWRKY19 + PtrMYB074 heterodimer --(transcriptional activation via W-box in PtrbHLH186 promoter)--> PtrbHLH186. Presence of functional PtrWRKY19 is required for PtrMYB074 association with PtrbHLH186 in vivo.
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: R2R3-MYB transcription factor (woody dicot-specific MYB TF).
Plant_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Nucleus; PtrMYB074-YFPN fused protein shows YFP fluorescence co-localized with nuclear marker H2A-1:mCherry in SDX protoplasts (BiFC).
Plant_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Forms heterodimers with PtrWRKY19 (identified by yeast two-hybrid and confirmed by BiFC in SDX protoplasts); also interacts with PtrMYB021 and many other TFs (54 total interactors identified in Y2H screen).
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Functions within stem-differentiating xylem (SDX) of P. trichocarpa as part of a wood-formation transcriptional regulatory network.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Wood formation and secondary xylem development via a PtrMYB074–PtrWRKY19–PtrbHLH186 transcriptional regulatory module.
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: wood formation (secondary xylem development), secondary cell wall and lignin biosynthesis, vessel and fibre cell development
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: ChIP-qPCR shows ~2-fold enrichment of the W-box-containing PtrbHLH186 promoter fragment in PtrMYB074-FLAG overexpressors vs wild-type; co-overexpression of PtrMYB074 and PtrWRKY19 in SDX protoplasts causes up to ~4-fold increase in PtrbHLH186 transcript compared with control, whereas PtrMYB074 alone has only a slight effect.
Plant_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: Loss-of-function of PtrMYB074 (CRISPR-Cas9 mutants ptrmyb074-1, -2, -3) reduces PtrbHLH186 transcript levels in xylem, indicating impaired activation of downstream lignification and vessel-development responses mediated by PtrbHLH186.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Populus trichocarpa (genotype Nisqually-1) wild-type plants; SDX protoplasts; OE-PtrMYB074-FLAG transgenic lines; CRISPR-Cas9 ptrmyb074 mutants; SDX tissues of 4–6-month-old plants.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: Yeast two-hybrid screening against 221 xylem-abundant TFs; BiFC in SDX protoplasts; generation of PtrMYB074-FLAG overexpression lines; ChIP-seq and ChIP-qPCR; EMSA with recombinant proteins; CRISPR-Cas9 knockout and genotyping; transient overexpression in SDX protoplasts with RT-qPCR analysis.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Protein–protein interaction assays (large-scale yeast two-hybrid with PtrMYB074N as bait; BiFC in SDX protoplasts); ChIP-seq and FLAG ChIP-qPCR in PtrMYB074-FLAG overexpression lines; EMSA with PtrMYB074 and PtrWRKY19; CRISPR-Cas9 knockout mutants (ptrmyb074) combined with ChIP-qPCR and RT-qPCR of PtrbHLH186; overexpression in SDX protoplasts.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Previously identified as a woody dicot-specific MYB transcription factor in a PtrSND1-directed transcriptional regulatory network for wood formation; in this study used as bait (full-length and N-terminal PtrMYB074N) in a yeast two-hybrid screen against 221 xylem-abundant P. trichocarpa transcription factors to identify interacting partners including PtrWRKY19.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: As a woody dicot-specific regulator at the top of a PtrMYB074–PtrWRKY19–PtrbHLH186 module, modulation of PtrMYB074 activity offers a way to tune the activation of PtrbHLH186 and thereby adjust lignin amount and composition, vessel number and size, and associated drought-tolerance traits in Populus wood, enabling breeding or engineering strategies for optimized growth vs. wood property trade-offs.

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: PtrWRKY19 is one of several PtrWRKY proteins (PtrWRKY12/13/19/25) that interact with PtrMYB074; these WRKYs form a subgroup and may share similar functions in wood formation, but PtrWRKY19 is the focus here due to its high xylem specificity and abundance.
Plant_Genes.Omics_Data: ChIP-seq of PtrWRKY19-FLAG in SDX tissue (four libraries: ChIP-DNA and input DNA, two biological replicates each) mapped to P. trichocarpa genome v.3.0; data deposited in NCBI SRA under SRR15246566–SRR15246573.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Serves as the DNA-binding component that targets PtrbHLH186 via a W-box in its promoter and, together with PtrMYB074, is essential for activation of PtrbHLH186 expression in xylem, thereby enabling downstream increases in lignification, altered vessel development and enhanced drought tolerance mediated by PtrbHLH186.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: WRKY transcription factor that directly recognizes and binds a W-box (TTGACT) motif in the PtrbHLH186 promoter, as shown by ChIP-qPCR and EMSA. Dimerization with PtrMYB074 leads to formation of a protein–DNA complex with two shifted bands in EMSA and is required for functional transactivation of PtrbHLH186; binding of PtrWRKY19 alone to the promoter is insufficient for activation.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Acts with PtrMYB074 in a PtrMYB074–PtrWRKY19–PtrbHLH186 module; directly binds W-box in PtrbHLH186 promoter and recruits PtrMYB074, forming a heterodimer required for transcriptional activation of PtrbHLH186 in secondary xylem.
Plant_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Regulation of lignin and secondary cell wall biosynthesis by directly binding the PtrbHLH186 promoter and, together with PtrMYB074, activating PtrbHLH186, which controls monolignol pathway genes.
Plant_Genes.Upstream_or_Downstream_Regulation: PtrWRKY19 --(direct DNA binding to W-box in promoter, ChIP-seq, ChIP-qPCR, EMSA)--> PtrbHLH186; PtrWRKY19 --(protein–protein interaction)--> PtrMYB074, recruiting PtrMYB074 to the PtrbHLH186 promoter; PtrWRKY19 + PtrMYB074 heterodimer --(activation)--> PtrbHLH186. Loss of PtrWRKY19 abolishes PtrMYB074 association with the PtrbHLH186 promoter in vivo.
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: WRKY transcription factor; binds W-box (TTGACT) motif in target promoters.
Plant_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Nucleus; PtrWRKY19-YFPC fused protein shows BiFC YFP signal co-localized with nuclear marker H2A-1:mCherry in SDX protoplasts when co-expressed with PtrMYB074-YFPN.
Plant_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Interacts with PtrMYB074 (heterodimerization) identified by yeast two-hybrid and confirmed in vivo by BiFC in SDX protoplasts.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Most highly expressed and xylem-specific PtrMYB074 interactor among 54 identified TF partners, based on RNA-seq of xylem, phloem, leaf and shoot tissues.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Role of a WRKY transcription factor in wood-formation transcriptional regulatory networks via interaction with PtrMYB074 and regulation of PtrbHLH186.
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: wood formation (secondary xylem development), lignin biosynthesis, vessel and fibre cell development
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: ChIP-qPCR in OE-PtrWRKY19-FLAG plants shows ~8-fold enrichment of the W-box-containing PtrbHLH186 promoter fragment vs wild-type; EMSA confirms strong binding to W-box with competition abolished by mutated W-box; co-overexpression with PtrMYB074 in SDX protoplasts yields ~4-fold increase in PtrbHLH186 transcript compared with control, whereas PtrWRKY19 alone has no significant effect; in ptrwrky19-3, PtrMYB074 ChIP enrichment at PtrbHLH186 promoter drops from ~2-fold (WT) to background.
Plant_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: CRISPR-Cas9 ptrwrky19 mutants (including homozygous ptrwrky19-3) show reduced PtrbHLH186 transcript levels in xylem and loss of detectable PtrMYB074 association with the PtrbHLH186 promoter in ChIP-qPCR, indicating disruption of the regulatory pathway.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Populus trichocarpa (genotype Nisqually-1) wild-type plants; SDX protoplasts; OE-PtrWRKY19-FLAG transgenic lines; CRISPR-Cas9 ptrwrky19 mutants; SDX tissues of 4–6-month-old plants.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: Yeast two-hybrid screen; BiFC in SDX protoplasts; generation of PtrWRKY19-FLAG overexpression lines; ChIP-seq and ChIP-qPCR; EMSA with recombinant PtrWRKY19 and mutated W-box competitors; CRISPR-Cas9 gene editing and mutant identification; transient overexpression in SDX protoplasts followed by RT-qPCR.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast two-hybrid and BiFC with PtrMYB074 for interaction; generation of PtrWRKY19-FLAG overexpression lines; ChIP-seq and FLAG ChIP-qPCR in SDX; EMSA to test binding to W-box and mutated W-box; CRISPR-Cas9 knockout mutants (ptrwrky19) combined with ChIP-qPCR using anti-PtrMYB074 antibody and RT-qPCR of PtrbHLH186; transient overexpression in SDX protoplasts.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Identified in a yeast two-hybrid screen using PtrMYB074N as bait against 221 xylem-abundant P. trichocarpa TFs; selected as the most highly expressed and xylem-specific PtrMYB074 interactor for detailed functional analysis.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: PtrWRKY19 provides a DNA-binding entry point for controlling the PtrMYB074–PtrWRKY19–PtrbHLH186 axis. Manipulating PtrWRKY19 expression or activity, alone or together with PtrMYB074, could be used to fine-tune PtrbHLH186 activation, allowing modification of lignin content/composition and xylem anatomy to improve wood properties and potentially drought resilience in Populus breeding or engineering programs.

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: G-lignin enrichment in OE-PtrbHLH186-L4 correlates with enhanced vessel development, consistent with G-type lignin association with vessel elements in Populus. OE-PtrbHLH186-L4 serves as a useful system to study co-regulation of growth, cell-type specification in wood formation and stress tolerance.
Plant_Genes.Omics_Data: Multi-omics integrative model of monolignol biosynthesis applied to RT-qPCR-derived expression of 22 pathway genes from SDX of OE-PtrbHLH186-L4 and WT plants to predict S and G lignin subunit levels; predictions (R^2 = 0.89) compared with measured lignin composition. ChIP-seq data for upstream regulators PtrMYB074 and PtrWRKY19 available in NCBI SRA (SRR15246566–SRR15246573).
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression in Populus trichocarpa leads to retarded plant growth (reduced height, internode number and stem basal diameter), early and enhanced lignification with ~20% higher total lignin and ~20% higher G-lignin, reduced S- and H-lignin and polysaccharides, increased number of vessel elements with smaller lumen and thicker walls and more fibres with thicker secondary walls, resulting in a larger total vessel lumen area and markedly improved drought tolerance.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: Nuclear bHLH TF that, when overexpressed, transregulates multiple monolignol biosynthetic genes, increasing G-lignin and total lignin and decreasing S and H lignin as well as carbohydrate content, while altering vessel and fibre development; regulation is driven by upstream activation from the PtrMYB074–PtrWRKY19 heterodimer.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Core downstream effector of the PtrMYB074–PtrWRKY19 dimer; its promoter is bound at a W-box by PtrWRKY19, with PtrMYB074 recruited via dimerization, and co-expression of PtrMYB074 and PtrWRKY19 is required to strongly activate PtrbHLH186 transcription in SDX. Activated PtrbHLH186 then modulates a network of monolignol pathway genes and xylem cell-type development.
Plant_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Monolignol and lignin biosynthesis pathway: overexpression upregulates monolignol biosynthetic genes (including PtrPAL2, PtrPAL4, PtrCCoAOMT2, PtrCCoAOMT3, PtrCCR2, PtrCAD1) leading to increased total lignin and guaiacyl (G) lignin with reduced syringyl (S) and p-hydroxyphenyl (H) lignin.
Plant_Genes.Upstream_or_Downstream_Regulation: Upstream: PtrWRKY19 --(direct W-box binding)--> PtrbHLH186; PtrWRKY19 + PtrMYB074 heterodimer --(transcriptional activation)--> PtrbHLH186. Downstream: Overexpression of PtrbHLH186 in SDX upregulates multiple monolignol biosynthetic genes, including PtrPAL2, PtrPAL4, PtrCCoAOMT2, PtrCCoAOMT3, PtrCCR2 and PtrCAD1, leading to increased G-lignin and total lignin and reduced S-lignin.
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: basic helix-loop-helix (bHLH) transcription factor family protein.
Plant_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Nucleus; 35S-PtrbHLH186-sGFP fusion in SDX protoplasts shows GFP signals co-localized with nuclear marker H2A-1:mCherry, indicating nuclear localization associated with wood-forming cells.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Xylem-abundant and xylem-specific among the common TF targets of PtrMYB074 and PtrWRKY19; overexpressed lines show 8–10-fold higher PtrbHLH186 transcript levels in stem-differentiating xylem than wild-type.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Drought stress imposed on 4-month-old plants by withholding water until soil relative water content decreased from 60% to 13%, followed by rehydration to assess survival.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Function of a bHLH transcription factor downstream of PtrMYB074–PtrWRKY19 in controlling secondary xylem development, lignin biosynthesis, vessel anatomy and drought tolerance.
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: secondary xylem development and wood formation, lignin content and composition, vessel and fibre cell morphology, plant growth rate, drought tolerance
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: Expression: four independent OE-PtrbHLH186 lines have 8–10-fold higher PtrbHLH186 transcript abundance in SDX than WT. Wood composition (OE-PtrbHLH186-L4 vs WT, g per 100 g dry extractive-free wood): total lignin increases from 21.81 ± 0.30 to 26.25 ± 1.12 (~+20%); acid-insoluble lignin from 18.25 ± 0.39 to 23.13 ± 1.14; acid-soluble lignin slightly decreases from 3.56 ± 0.09 to 3.12 ± 0.05. Glucose decreases from 54.36 ± 0.13 to 36.19 ± 1.83 (~−33%); total carbohydrate from 67.33 ± 0.29 to 46.92 ± 2.85 (~−30%); C:L ratio from 3.10 ± 0.04 to 1.78 ± 0.04. Lignin composition (percentage of total lignin): S-lignin decreases from 61.64 ± 0.57% to 57.85 ± 0.12%; G-lignin increases from 30.19 ± 0.39% to 36.16 ± 0.17% (~+20% relative); H-lignin decreases from 8.16 ± 0.72% to 5.99 ± 0.08%; S/G ratio declines from 2.04 ± 0.03 to 1.60 ± 0.01. Drought survival: after soil RWC is reduced from 60% to 13% and plants are rehydrated, survival is ~22% in WT and ~89% in OE-PtrbHLH186-L4.
Plant_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: OE-PtrbHLH186 plants show: (1) stunted growth maintained over at least 4 months; (2) early and more intense lignification during stem development; (3) doubled number of vessel elements with significantly smaller lumen area, shorter vessel length and thicker secondary cell walls; (4) increased number of shorter fibre cells with thicker secondary cell walls; (5) increased total vessel lumen area per cross-sectional area; (6) strong drought-tolerant phenotypes with much higher survival after severe soil drying and rehydration.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Populus trichocarpa (genotype Nisqually-1) wild-type plants; OE-PtrbHLH186 transgenic lines (L1–L4) generated by Agrobacterium-mediated transformation with 35S:PtrbHLH186 in pBI121; SDX tissues and protoplasts; 4-month-old plants grown in glasshouse for growth, wood chemistry, anatomy and drought assays.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: Generation of 35S:PtrbHLH186 overexpression constructs and transgenic lines; RT-qPCR of PtrbHLH186 and monolignol pathway genes; subcellular localization using sGFP fusion in SDX protoplasts; histological staining (Safranin O/Fast Green, toluidine blue) and microscopy; scanning electron microscopy of xylem cell morphology and wall thickness; quantitative wood chemistry for lignin and carbohydrate content and lignin composition; drought treatment reducing soil RWC from 60% to 13% followed by rehydration and survival scoring; multi-omics modeling of lignin S and G subunits based on RT-qPCR data.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Stable overexpression of PtrbHLH186 under the CaMV 35S promoter in P. trichocarpa (OE-PtrbHLH186 lines) with RT-qPCR confirmation; phenotypic analysis of plant growth; histological staining of stem cross and longitudinal sections; scanning electron microscopy of xylem cells; quantitative wood chemistry (lignin and carbohydrate content and lignin composition); drought treatment and survival assay; multi-omics integrative modeling of monolignol biosynthesis using RT-qPCR-based expression profiles of 22 pathway genes.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Identified by ChIP-seq as one of 15 common promoter targets co-bound by PtrMYB074 and PtrWRKY19 (within 3-kb upstream region); among the two common TF targets (PtrbHLH186 and PtrVCM2), PtrbHLH186 was selected because it is more xylem-abundant and xylem-specific, and its function had not previously been studied.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: PtrbHLH186 overexpression produces wood with higher lignin (especially G-lignin) and lower carbohydrate content together with more, smaller vessel elements and enhanced drought tolerance. These traits suggest that targeted manipulation of PtrbHLH186 or its upstream regulators could be used to generate Populus trees with improved drought resilience and tailored lignin composition for specific industrial uses, while carefully managing the trade-off with reduced growth.
